evidenceChecked: 11c5b8b39e75cda2bc349c71758528f27fa0eacf:content/010-orientation.md through content/110-references.md in declared scene order
Cited repository blobs at 76882799c7614eeb9307687bceab33896a79bb13 plus CONTENT-LICENSE.md and RIGHTS-POLICY.md for source scope, rights, and agent access
Targeted node scripts/validate-lessons.mjs: one production pack and one excluded specimen valid before review artifact creation
Time-boxed rendered audit of all 11 scenes: keyboard navigation/focus, semantic accessibility tree, reduced motion, reading/print flow, and 22 reflow cases at 640 px and 390 px
Static pack tree and active-content/privacy/secret/path scan; all candidate entries are regular blobs and no executable lesson asset or remote embed is present
Original-synthesis comparison across learner-facing prose, assessment, and glossary against all four cited sources; no shared sequence of eight or more normalized words was found

findings.finding: The complete classify–justify–route operation is available in semantic source-order prose, headed tables, and untimed text or choice responses. The lesson supplies keyboard, speech-to-text, scribe, ordered-sentence table, and format-change routes without changing the reasoning standard; it does not depend on color, position, sound, motion, hover, dragging, handwriting, or speed. The rendered candidate exposed one main landmark, one visible level-one scene heading, table and header semantics, visible keyboard focus, working keyboard frame navigation, reduced-motion recognition, continuous reading and print flow, and no page-level overflow across all 11 scenes at 640 px and 390 px.

All four sources are repository-owned, version-pinned records used on a facts-only/original-expression basis with dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found declarations. The cited content is covered by the project's CC BY 4.0 content license, no source is marked human-only, and no third-party expression, asset, media, or dataset is incorporated. The accountable author, agent system, provider, model, CLI version, isolated author run, and material-instructions digest agree across lesson.json and ATTRIBUTION.md. A targeted expression comparison found no shared source sequence of eight or more normalized words in the learner-facing prose, assessment, or glossary.

The transfer task expressly excludes names, grades, disability details, health information, and other private data, while every worked record is labelled fictional or self-generated. The pack contains no images, diagrams, structured molecules, media, executable code, raw HTML, scripts, remote embeds, active SVG, unsafe TeX, traversal path, secret, patient record, learner record, or copyrighted answer-bank material. Declared paths remain pack-local and every candidate entry is a regular file.

limitations: This is an accessibility-and-rights policy review, not legal advice. Copyright, privacy, consent, and license conclusions may vary by jurisdiction; qualified counsel remains necessary for jurisdiction-specific reliance or a rights dispute.

The interface audit was time-boxed and used headless Chrome, DOM/accessibility-tree inspection, keyboard operation, reduced-motion emulation, print media, and narrow/reflow viewports. It did not include a human screen-reader session, disabled-user study, hardware switch device, speech-recognition product, or formal legal conformance certification.

The generated production lesson detail currently declares schemaVersion 2 while the repository reader accepts schemaVersion 3. Direct loading therefore failed before candidate content rendered; the bounded audit normalized only that discriminator in memory and made no lesson or repository change. This is a repository renderer integration limitation, not a defect in this candidate's schemaVersion 3 source.

The founding-stage policy permits the same disclosed accountable principal to operate author and review agents. This review used a fresh agent run and did not reuse the author run, but it is not an independent human or organizational rights review.

The instructions digest covers exactly the discloseable reviewer task paragraph supplied in the current user message, encoded as UTF-8 without a trailing newline. It excludes hidden provider instructions and the repository authorities read during the run.
